methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $18M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Dendra Systems is a Series A-stage climate and energy technology company that has raised $18M to date. The peer universe for valuation comprises 3 private comparables in the same sector and stage (Graphyte at $200M, Fourth Power at $150M, and Steady Energy at $110M), supplemented by a broader set of 8 private peers and 1 public comparable (LNZA).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $18M total raised as a baseline and applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement, yielding $36M. Peer-median analysis anchors to the median valuation of the 3 same-sector, same-stage comparables at $150M, with a range spanning $110M to $200M. These methods produced divergent signals, reflecting both the company's early capital raise and the elevated valuations observed across its climate-tech peer cohort.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $31M to $172M, with a median of $93M. The wide dispersion—driven by the 4.2x gap between stage-progression and peer-median inputs—underscores material uncertainty regarding whether Dendra's competitive position and traction align with peer valuation multiples or whether the peer set reflects sector-wide overvaluation. Key uncertainties include current revenue run-rate, customer concentration, and the path to cash-flow breakeven relative to peer trajectories.
